questions.q: A particle executing simple harmonic motion is at a point where its velocity is half of its maximum velocity. Determine the fraction of its maximum displacement from its mean position.

A 2 kg mass is attached to a horizontal massless spring. When the mass is displaced by 2 m from its mean position and released, it executes S.H.M. with a frequency of 0.5 Hz. Find the spring constant.
A particle is performing S.H.M. on a straight line with amplitude A. If the total energy of the particle is 12 J and maximum velocity is 4 m/s, find the angular frequency of the particle.
A particle of mass 0.1 kg executes S.H.M. with a time period of 0.1 s. Determine the angular frequency of the particle.
A body is executing S.H.M. with a frequency of 5 Hz and amplitude of 20 cm. Determine the maximum acceleration of the body.
A particle is performing S.H.M. with a time period of 2 s and amplitude of 3 m. Determine the total energy of the particle.

A mass of 0.25 kg is attached to a horizontal spring fixed at the bottom of a smooth inclined plane. The length of the spring is 0.2 m when the mass is on the inclined plane. When the mass is displaced by 0.1 m from the equilibrium position, the spring is stretched to a length of 0.32 m. Determine the frequency of oscillation.
A particle executes simple harmonic motion with an amplitude of 12 cm and a frequency of 3 Hz. If the total energy of the particle is 72 J, determine its maximum acceleration.
A particle is performing S.H.M. with a frequency of 10 Hz and amplitude of 5 m. Determine the angular frequency of the particle.
A simple pendulum of length 1.5 m has a time period of 2 s. Determine the acceleration due to gravity.
